market_note: Circle shares dropped 17.55% after 140+ firms (Visa, Stripe, BlackRock, Coinbase) launched the rival Open USD stablecoin, as GENIUS Act final rules loom on July 18.

stablecoin_items.headline: Visa, Stripe, BlackRock, Coinbase launch Open USD (OUSD) stablecoin
stablecoin_items.summary: 140+ companies including Mastercard, BNY and Standard Chartered launched OUSD on Solana with free issuance/redemption and reserve revenue shared back to distributors, undercutting Circle and Tether's retained-yield model.

stablecoin_items.headline: GENIUS Act final stablecoin rules due July 18
stablecoin_items.summary: Six federal agencies are finalizing GENIUS Act rules after comment periods closed June 9; non-bank fintechs like Stripe and Block must raise $5M in dedicated capital and charter a stablecoin bank or exit the market.

stablecoin_items.headline: USDT market cap hits all-time high near $188B, widening lead over USDC
stablecoin_items.summary: Tether's dominance has grown since Circle was criticized for slow freezing of USDC linked to the Drift Protocol exploit; USDC sits at roughly $78.25B.

stablecoin_items.headline: Regulators propose bank-grade KYC rules for stablecoin issuers
stablecoin_items.summary: Five US regulators jointly proposed stricter KYC requirements under the GENIUS Act, putting both Circle's USDC and Tether's USDT under closer scrutiny.

hack_items.headline: Syscoin Bridge exploited for ~$10M
hack_items.summary: An attacker exploited a proof-validation flaw to mint roughly 5 billion unauthorized SYS tokens, one of the larger DeFi bridge incidents of June 2026.

hack_items.headline: Humanity Protocol drained of $31-36M via compromised founder key
hack_items.summary: Attackers gained control after a private key linked to founder Terence Kwok was compromised on a malware-infected machine, then minted and drained the project's H token across two networks.

hack_items.headline: H1 2026 sets record for hack count, North Korea tied to 66% of stolen funds
hack_items.summary: TRM Labs counted 207 crypto hacking incidents in H1 2026 with losses near $972M-$1B; roughly two-thirds of stolen funds are attributed to North Korea-linked actors.

hack_items.headline: Wasabi Protocol drained of $4.5M in admin key compromise
hack_items.summary: An apparent admin key compromise let attackers siphon $4.5M from the protocol, part of a wider pattern of private-key and key-management failures driving 2026 losses.
